pages.text: $(o)"The eighteen threads of Transcendence may all be held by one — but none lived long enough to weave them all."$()$(br2)Every ancient mage chose a $(l)path$() — a way to live alongside Transcendence's power.$(br2)Seven schools survive in writing, each with its own element-affinity and worldview.

$(l)Pyromancer$() — burning is truth$(br)$(l)Cryomancer$() — stillness is eternity$(br)$(l)Stormcaller$() — speed is victory$(br)$(l)Earthshaper$() — solidity is endurance$(br)$(l)Abysswalker$() — void is fact$(br)$(l)Arcanist$() — balance is wisdom$(br)$(l)Omnimancer$() — mastery of all is transcendence

Your school choice shapes your talent tree and element affinities.$(br2)$(l)Early game$(): pick the school matching your most-used spell element.$(br2)$(l)Late game$(): Omnimancer costs the most talent points but unlocks every branch.
